sprint: Magic Card -- Printed Typed-Code Now Matches Recall's Own Check
task: Fixes the adjacent bug the previous sprint disclosed but left open. card.id (an internal, locally-minted random string) was being printed on the card as its human-readable code, but recall_magic_card()'s own typed-code branch checks a completely different value -- upper(constellation || lpad(serial_no::text,5,'0')), e.g. CYGNUS00042 -- built from a server-assigned serial number the client had never learned. Typing the printed code back in would always have failed. Fixed by having js/magicCard.js's _pushIdentitySnapshot (the one shared function claim()/touch()/rename() all funnel their cloud writes through) chain .select('serial_no,constellation') onto its upsert() and capture the result into a new local recallCode field matching the RPC's exact format -- verified directly against a mocked response, not assumed. Because every later touch() re-runs this same push, a card whose first sync failed self-heals the moment any later push succeeds, no migration needed. js/magicCardArt.js's drawFront/drawBack now print recallCode instead of card.id, with an honest 'Code syncing...' fallback (matching the existing 'No sky to show yet' precedent) for the brief window between claim() returning and the async push resolving, rather than ever printing a code that would fail if typed back in. No RPC/schema change needed -- the gap was purely client-side. Verified via a direct Node harness exercising the real promise chain end-to-end, plus full regression across all four existing Magic Card Playwright suites and both goldenBuild.js suites -- all passing unchanged.
